criteria: The agent has read Section 2 completely to understand the empirical and theoretical analysis connecting latent space structure (GMM modes) to diffusion model training difficulty and generation quality.
The agent has read Theorem 2.1 to understand that achieving the same generation quality O(T epsilon^2) requires training sample size scaling as O(K^4) with the number of GMM modes K.
The agent has read Section 3.1 completely to understand the MAETok encoder-decoder architecture with learnable latent tokens and ViT backbone.
The agent has read Section 3.2 completely to understand the mask modeling approach, token masking at the encoder, and auxiliary shallow decoders.
The agent has read Section 3.3 completely to understand the pixel decoder fine-tuning stage that recovers reconstruction fidelity after mask modeling.
The agent has read Table 7 and Appendix B.1 to understand the complete training configuration of MAETok tokenizer.
The agent has read Tables 8 and 9 and Appendix B.2 to understand the training configuration of SiT-XL and LightningDiT diffusion models.
The agent has read Table 1 to check all ablation experiment settings comparing mask modeling variants, reconstruction targets, mask ratios, and auxiliary decoder depths.
The agent has read Table 2 to check the system-level generation comparison on ImageNet 256x256.
The agent has read Table 3 to check the system-level generation comparison on ImageNet 512x512.
The agent has read Table 6 to understand the component ablation study evaluating the individual contribution of mask modeling, learnable tokens, and auxiliary decoders.
The agent has read Appendix B.3 to understand the GMM fitting pipeline including latent flattening, PCA with >90% explained variance, normalization, and NLL evaluation.
The plan includes training separate AE, VAE, and MAETok tokenizers under identical settings for GMM latent space analysis and diffusion loss comparison.

The plan includes the GMM fitting pipeline: flatten latents to (N, H*C), apply PCA to select dimensions with >90% explained variance, standardize, fit GMM with varying component counts, and report NLL.

The plan includes the 1D tokenizer design with L=128 learnable latent tokens of hidden dimension H=32, a ViT-Base encoder (hidden dim 768, 12 heads, 12 layers, patch size 16), and a ViT-Base pixel decoder of the same capacity.
The plan includes implementing token masking at the encoder with a random mask ratio of 40-60% and replacing masked image patch tokens with a learnable mask token m.
The plan includes three auxiliary shallow decoders (each with 3 transformer layers) predicting HOG features, DINOv2-Large features, and SigCLIP-Large features of masked tokens.

The plan includes the two-stage training: first training with mask modeling for 500K steps, then freezing the encoder and fine-tuning only the pixel decoder for 50K steps with mask ratio linearly decreasing from 60% to 0%.

The plan includes training the MAETok tokenizer with AdamW optimizer (LR=1e-4, weight decay=1e-4, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.95), global batch size 512, cosine LR schedule, 10K warmup steps, and DINOv2-S discriminator.

The plan includes training SiT-XL (675M parameters, hidden dim 1152, 16 heads, 28 layers, patch size 1) with AdamW (LR=1e-4, weight decay=0, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999), batch size 256, and 4M training steps.

The ablation studies for mask modeling variants (AE, VAE, AE+MM, AE+MM+FT), reconstruction targets (pixel, HOG, DINO, CLIP, combined), mask ratios, and auxiliary decoder depths are planned using SiT-L for 400K steps.
The plan includes main generation experiments training SiT-XL for 4M steps and LightningDiT for 400K steps on MAETok latent space for both 256x256 and 512x512 ImageNet benchmarks.
The plan includes tokenizer reconstruction evaluation (rFID, PSNR, SSIM) on ImageNet and MS-COCO validation sets and linear probing accuracy on flattened latent representations.

The GMM fitting pipeline has been implemented: flatten latent representations to (N, H*C), apply PCA selecting dimensions that explain >90% variance, standardize the data, and fit GMM to report negative log-likelihood losses.

The encoder has been implemented as Equation 4: h = E([x; z]), where image patch embeddings x (obtained by dividing input image into N patches of size P=16 mapped to dimension D=768) and L=128 learnable latent tokens z are concatenated, and only the latent token outputs h of dimension H=32 are used.

The decoder has been implemented as Equation 5: x_hat = D([e; h]), where N learnable image tokens e of dimension H are concatenated with latent representations h, and only the image token outputs are passed through a linear layer to reconstruct pixel values.

The position encoding has been implemented with 2D Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) applied to image patch tokens x at the encoder and image tokens e at the decoder, and 1D absolute position embedding (APE) applied to latent tokens z and h.

The tokenizer training objective has been implemented as Equation 6: L = L_recon + lambda1 * L_percep + lambda2 * L_adv, using pixel-wise MSE reconstruction loss, perceptual loss, and adversarial loss with lambda1=1.0 and lambda2=0.4.

The token masking mechanism has been implemented: randomly select a ratio (40-60%) of image patch tokens according to binary masking indicator M and replace them with a shared learnable mask token m before feeding to the encoder, while keeping all L latent tokens unmasked.

Each auxiliary decoder D_aux^j has been implemented as Equation 7: y_hat^j = D_aux^j([e^j; h]; theta), where decoder-specific tokens e^j are concatenated with latent representations h and the output predicts target features y^j of dimension D^j.
The masked reconstruction loss has been implemented as Equation 8: L_mask = sum_j || M ⊗ (y_hat^j - y^j) ||^2_2, computing MSE loss only at masked token positions for each auxiliary target j.
The extraction of HOG features from masked image regions has been implemented as a reconstruction target for auxiliary decoder training.
The extraction of DINOv2-Large features from masked image regions has been implemented as a reconstruction target for auxiliary decoder training.
The extraction of SigCLIP-Large features from masked image regions has been implemented as a reconstruction target for auxiliary decoder training.
Three auxiliary shallow decoders, each with 3 transformer layers, have been implemented for ImageNet training to predict HOG, DINOv2-Large, and SigCLIP-Large features of masked tokens.
The pixel decoder fine-tuning stage has been implemented: freeze the encoder weights, fine-tune only the pixel decoder using Equation 6 loss, and discard all auxiliary decoders for this stage.
The mask ratio annealing during pixel decoder fine-tuning has been implemented: linearly decreasing the mask ratio from 60% to 0% over 50K fine-tuning iterations.
The MAETok tokenizer has been trained with AdamW optimizer (LR=1e-4, weight decay=1e-4, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.95), global batch size 512, cosine LR schedule, and 10K warmup steps.
The DINOv2-S discriminator has been integrated for adversarial training with weight 0.4 and adaptive weighting, starting at 30K steps, with LeCAM regularization weight 0.001.
Horizontal flip and center crop augmentations have been applied during tokenizer training on ImageNet.
SiT-L with 458M parameters has been implemented with patch size 1 and 1D position embedding for use in ablation studies.
SiT-XL with 675M parameters has been implemented with hidden dimension 1152, 16 attention heads, 28 layers, and patch size 1 with 1D sinusoidal position embedding.
SiT-XL has been configured for training with AdamW optimizer (LR=1e-4, weight decay=0.0, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999), global batch size 256, constant LR schedule, and Euler-Maruyama diffusion sampler.

LightningDiT with 675M parameters has been implemented with patch size 1, 1D RoPE position embedding, AdamW (LR=2e-4, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.95), global batch size 1024, and cosine loss as additional training loss.
Generation evaluation has been configured to run 250 inference steps using Euler-Maruyama (SiT-XL) or Euler (LightningDiT) sampler and compute gFID and IS on 50K generated samples.

The classifier-free guidance (CFG) inference has been implemented with CFG interval support: scale 1.9 with interval [0, 0.75] for 256x256 SiT-XL and scale 1.5 with interval [0, 0.7] for 512x512 SiT-XL.
The tokenizer reconstruction evaluation pipeline has been implemented to compute rFID, PSNR, and SSIM on ImageNet and MS-COCO validation sets.
The linear probing evaluation has been implemented by training a linear classifier on flattened latent representations and reporting ImageNet classification accuracy.
An additional auxiliary decoder for BPE text caption index prediction has been implemented for the LAION-COCO 512x512 tokenizer variant.
The code for AE and VAE baseline tokenizer training (without mask modeling) has been implemented under the same training recipe and latent dimension as MAETok for fair comparison.
The GMM analysis has been executed: training GMM on ImageNet latent spaces of AE, VAE, VAVAE, and MAETok with varying component counts (50, 100, 200), comparing NLL losses to determine mode count.
SiT-L has been trained on the latent spaces of AE, VAE, VAVAE, and MAETok for 400K iterations with AdamW (constant LR=1e-4, no weight decay) for diffusion loss and gFID comparison.
All mask modeling ablation experiments (AE, VAE, AE+MM, AE+MM+FT) have been executed by training each tokenizer for 250K steps followed by SiT-L for 400K steps on 256x256 ImageNet.
All reconstruction target ablation experiments (pixel, HOG, DINO, CLIP, combined) have been executed with MAETok tokenizers followed by SiT-L evaluation.
All mask ratio ablation experiments with different low-high mask ratio pairs have been executed with HOG target and no fine-tuning, followed by SiT-L evaluation.
All auxiliary decoder depth ablation experiments (linear, 1, 3, 6, 12 blocks) with combined reconstruction targets have been executed followed by SiT-L evaluation.
All component ablation experiments (7 combinations of mask modeling, learnable tokens, and auxiliary decoders) have been executed and evaluated with SiT-L.
SiT-XL has been trained for 4M steps on MAETok latent space for 256x256 ImageNet generation successfully without errors.
LightningDiT has been trained for 400K steps on MAETok latent space for 256x256 ImageNet generation successfully without errors.
SiT-XL and LightningDiT have been trained on MAETok latent space for 512x512 ImageNet generation successfully without errors.
The unconditional generation experiment using SiT-L on different MAETok variants has been executed successfully, measuring gFID and IS without class conditioning.
The execution outcomes match Figure 2a: VAVAE achieves lower NLL with fewer GMM components compared to AE and VAE, demonstrating its simpler latent space structure.
The execution outcomes match Figure 2b: MAETok achieves the lowest diffusion training loss (gFID=5.78) compared to AE (24.47), VAE (22.17), and VAVAE (13.65), aligning with having fewer GMM modes.

The execution outcomes match Table 1a: adding mask modeling (AE+MM) reduces gFID from 24.47 to 5.78 for AE, while further fine-tuning (AE+MM+FT) reduces rFID from 0.85 to 0.48 while maintaining gFID=5.69.
The execution outcomes match Table 1a: mask modeling only marginally improves VAE gFID (from 22.17 to 18.17), significantly less than its effect on AE.
The execution outcomes match Table 1b: using combined reconstruction targets (pixel+HOG+DINO+CLIP) achieves the best gFID=5.78, with DINO alone achieving gFID=6.24.

The execution outcomes match Table 1c: mask ratio range of 40-60% achieves gFID=17.18 with HOG target (without fine-tuning), and no masking (0-60%) achieves gFID=24.15, confirming the importance of mask ratio.
The execution outcomes match Table 1d: 3-block auxiliary decoders achieve the best trade-off (rFID=0.85, gFID=5.78), while linear (gFID=6.98) or too-deep (12-block: gFID=8.80) decoders underperform.

The execution outcomes match Table 6: all three components together achieve rFID=0.85 and gFID=5.78; removing the auxiliary decoder alone increases gFID to 17.18 and removing learnable tokens alone increases gFID to 6.85.

The execution outcomes match Figure 5a: tokenizers with more discriminative latent representations (higher linear probing accuracy) achieve lower gFID, with MAETok-DINOv2/CLIP variants achieving ~60% LP accuracy.
The execution outcomes match Figure 5b: MAETok achieves faster diffusion training convergence with significantly lower gFID at each training checkpoint compared to AE, VAE, and REPA baselines.
The execution outcomes match Table 4: MAETok achieves rFID=0.48, PSNR=23.61, SSIM=0.763 on 256x256 ImageNet and rFID=0.62, PSNR=22.18, SSIM=0.701 on 512x512 ImageNet using only 128 tokens.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: MAETok+SiT-XL achieves gFID=2.31 without CFG and gFID=1.67 with CFG on 256x256 ImageNet, outperforming REPA (gFID=5.90 without CFG).
The execution outcomes match Table 2: MAETok+LightningDiT achieves gFID=2.21 without CFG and gFID=1.73 with CFG on 256x256 ImageNet using only 128 tokens.
The execution outcomes match Table 3: MAETok+SiT-XL achieves gFID=2.79 without CFG and gFID=1.69 (IS=304.2) with CFG on 512x512 ImageNet, outperforming USiT-2B with only 675M parameters.
The execution outcomes match Table 3: MAETok+LightningDiT achieves gFID=2.56 without CFG and gFID=1.72 with CFG on 512x512 ImageNet.

The execution outcomes match Table 5: MAETok unconditional generation with all reconstruction targets achieves gFID=18.31 and IS=47.33 with SiT-L, significantly outperforming plain AE (gFID=59.02, IS=16.91).
The execution outcomes match Table 10: MAETok+SiT-XL achieves Precision=0.78 and Recall=0.62 without CFG on 256x256 ImageNet, comparable to competing diffusion-based models.
The execution outcomes match Table 13a: MAETok with 128 learnable latent tokens achieves rFID=0.48 and gFID=5.69, competitive with 256 tokens (rFID=0.37, gFID=5.05).

The execution outcomes match Table 13b: 2D RoPE achieves rFID=0.51 at 256x256 resolution and rFID=0.72 at 512x512, significantly better than APE (0.73 and 1.43), confirming its importance for multi-resolution generalization.
